what_it_does: Documents the ConceptV Platform UI kit, describing its creator-side dashboard role, layout zones, component catalog, and demo entry behavior.
observations: Defines the platform as a creator-side dashboard for architects, developers, and sales/marketing agencies packaging architectural visualisation projects into branded Virtual Hubs.
Specifies three fixed layout zones: a 280 px left rail, a top-right notification/avatar cluster inside the content column, and a floating bottom-right Chats pill.
Describes the content column sequence as bronze 36 px screen title, toolbar row, then the active surface.
Lists component files and screen files with short responsibility descriptions.
States that index.html mounts the full platform with Gallery as the default screen and sidebar navigation to Gallery, Calendar, Brand Kit, and Virtual Hub Settings.
standouts: The top-right cluster is explicitly scoped to the content column rather than the whole page.
The README ties visual styling tokens to components, including bronze titles, red count badge, gold-fill checkbox, soft-gold panels, and dashed upload states.
The component table gives a compact map of navigation, chrome, form, media, calendar, and floating chat pieces.
conventions: Uses a Markdown README structure with title, descriptive paragraph, Layout, Components, and Demo sections.
Uses a table to map file names to UI roles.
Uses PascalCase React component filenames and a screens/ subdirectory naming pattern.
Describes layout through fixed zones and explicit pixel measurements.
notes: This file is a concise handoff index for the ConceptV Platform UI kit structure, layout rules, and demo behavior.
questions: Which sidebar items beyond Gallery, Calendar, Brand Kit, and Virtual Hub Settings are implemented as navigable screens?
Are the visual tokens such as bronze and soft-gold defined elsewhere or only described here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents the platform UI kit, its layout, component inventory, and demo behavior.
apparent_themes: Creator dashboard for architectural visualisation packaging
Fixed application chrome with content-column focus
intention_signals: It seems intended to orient implementers or reviewers quickly around the platform dashboard's structure, expected component responsibilities, and default demo flow.
novelty: The file emphasizes a top-right cluster constrained to the content column and a colour-emoji sidebar navigation model within a professional creator dashboard.
contribution: Contributes a file-local ledger of platform UI kit responsibilities: the dashboard purpose, fixed layout zones, content sequencing, component inventory, and demo navigation expectations.
summary_for_embedding: This README documents the ConceptV Platform creator dashboard UI kit, including its fixed layout zones, toolbar/content flow, component file roles, and Gallery-default demo entry.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to brief a handoff reader on how the ConceptV Platform UI kit is organized and how its dashboard shell and screens should behave.
